mnemonics: Recall CONFUSION MATRIX for the four outcomes: True Positive, True Negative, False Positive, False Negative. Think: 'The Matrix of Truth vs. False Alarms'.
keyPoints: Accuracy is the ratio of correct predictions (TP+TN) to total predictions.
Precision measures how many of the positive predictions were actually correct (TP/(TP+FP)).
Recall (Sensitivity) measures how many of the actual positives were correctly identified (TP/(TP+FN)).
commonMistakes: Using accuracy as the sole metric for imbalanced datasets (e.g., 99% accuracy when 99% of data is one class).
Confusing Precision (correctness of positive predictions) with Recall (completeness of positive predictions).
